hints.Dominus: 'Dominus' is a noun — it's the subject of the sentence
hints.me: 'me' is a pronoun in the accusative case
hints.et: 'et' is a conjunction meaning 'and' — it doesn't appear here

hints.to lead: 'to lead' is 'ducere' — 'regere' means 'to rule' or 'to guide'

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'regere' would be 'regebat' — 'regit' lacks the -ba- marker
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'regere' would be 'rexit' — 'regit' uses the present stem
hints.Future: Future of 'regere' would be 'reget' — 'regit' has the present -i- vowel

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to drive/do)
hints.lego, legere, legi, lectum: These are the principal parts of 'legere' (to read/gather)
hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the principal parts of 'ducere' (to lead)

hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — 'Dominus' ends in -us, typical of 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns also end in -us but have genitive -us (e.g. 'manus, manus')

hints.Nominative: Nominative of the 1st person pronoun is 'ego'
hints.Dative: Dative of the 1st person pronoun is 'mihi'
hints.Ablative: 'me' can be ablative, but here it's the direct object of 'regit'
